name: Canine Atrial Septal Defect
description: An atrial septal defect (ASD) is a congenital hole in the wall separating the heart's upper chambers. Blood shunts between atria, overloading the right side of the heart. Small defects may be clinically silent; large ones cause heart failure.
symptoms: Signs of heart failure in severe cases
treatment_overview: Small ASDs may require no treatment. Larger defects in dogs with clinical signs can be closed via minimally invasive catheter-based occlusion or open-heart surgery. Medical management of heart failure is used otherwise.
